198 SPIRITUAL BRAVF:RY out His hand so the interests of the great powers will be such that they might be helpful. Every so often we will hear of something new. We will see the most incredible things, the most irrational things happening. But these events will occur and pass quickly.

Ecumenism, the European Union, one huge nation, one religion made to fit ~ these are the schemes of the devil. The Zionists are preparing someone to be the Messiah. For them the Messiah is a king who will reign here on earth. The Jehovah's Witnesses are also looking toward an earthly king. The Zionists will present someone and the Jehovah's Witnesses will accept him. They will say, "He is the one." There will be great confusion. In that confusion, everyone will be looking for a Messiah to save them. And then they will present sorne who will say, "l am the lmam, I am the fifth Buddha, I am the Christ expected by the Christians, 1 am the one expected by the Jehovah 's Witnesses, 1 am the Messiah of the Jews." He will have five "selves"...

When John the Evangelist says in the First Epistle, Little children... as you heard that Antichrist cometh, even now have there arisen many antichrists...,3 he does not mean that the anticipated Antichrist will be like the persecutors Maximinian or Diocletian. Rather, the anticipated Antichrist will, in sorne manner, be the incamate devil, who will present himself to the Jewish nation as the Messiah and will mislead the world. Difficult years are ahead; we will be tested very severely. The Christians will suffer great persecution. And, you see, people are not at all aware that we are livin~ during the signs of 3.1 Jn2:18.

The Signs of the Times 199 the times, that the sealing is already advancing. lt is as if nothing is happening. This is why the Sacred Scripture says that even the elect will be deceived. 4 Those without a good disposition will not be enlightened and will be misled during the years of apostasy. For whoever does not have divine Grace, does not have spiritual clarity, just like the devil. ~ Geronda, do the Zionists believe what is said about the Antichrist? ~ They would like to govem the whole world. To achieve their goal, they will use even sorcery and satanism. They see the worship of Satan as a power that will help them achieve their goals. They will seek to govem the world with satanic power. They do not consider God in their schemes. But will they be blessed by God in their efforts?

God will bring a lot of good out of this situation. Sorne other satanic theories at leas! held sway for about seventy years; but they won't even last for seven years. ~ Geronda, when 1 hear about the Antichrist, 1 feel a fear inside of me. ~ What do you fear? Will he be more terrible than the devil? He is aman after all. Saint Marina used to beat the devil, and Saint Justina dispelled so many demons. After all, we didn 't come in this world to have an easy life.
The Earthly King tJ{the.Jews A sign marking the fulfilment of the prophecies will be the destruction of the mosque of Ornar in Jerusalem.
They will tear it down to rebuild the Temple ofSolomon,

200 SPIRITUAL BRAVERY which, as they say, was built on that site. Ultimately the Zionists will install the Antichrist there as the Messiah. I have heard that the Jews are already preparing to rebuild the Temple of Solomon. - Geronda, how is it that Jews read the Old Testament but do not believe in Christ? - Why don't you go and ask the Jews? The Jews were always fanatical. They do understand, but it is their egoism that blinds them. Ifthey only had paid a little attention to the Sacred Scripture, no one would remain a Jew. - How did they interpret what they read? - Indeed, how did they and how do they interpret Sacred Scripture! They turn spiritual meanings into material ones. For example, you should see how they interpret Prophet lsaiah 's the desert shall blossom. 5 In order to show that the desert has indeed blossomed, they altered the course of a stream, built irrigation systems, planted gardens, banana trees, lemon trees, orange trees, they created orchards and gardens everywhere so they can say, The desert has blossomed! That is how they interpret everything. But the words of the Prophet actually refer to the spiritual renewal ofthe world through the Holy Spirit, through the Baptism of regeneration. -Are they now expecting the earthly king? -Yes, the Antichrist. The Rabbis know that the Messiah has already come and that they have crucified Him. I have learned from a person that when a Jew is dying, the Rabbi goes and whispers in his ear, "The Messiah has come." You see, their conscience seems to trouble them by a sense of culpability, and yet they do not humble themselves. • 5.1sa35:1-2..¡¡...

The Signs of the Times 201 - And what do they gain by saying that to the dying? - Nothing. They just say it because their conscience is troubling them and they think that by saying it to the dying they will be all right. - And other people don't hear this? -No, it is whispered in the ear. The Jewish youth have revolted against the Rabbis. "The Messiah has come," they say, "what Messiah are you waiting for?" In America there is a group ofyoung Jews who study Sacred Scripture historically and publish a journal in which they write, "The Messiah has come. We will send this joumal free of charge to whoever doesn 't believe that the Messiah has come, until they do believe. Whoever believes should send us their subscription fee to help us send the joumal to others so they will al so believe." -Are these people Jews? -Yes, they are Jews. -Ha ve they become Christians? - Well, for now it suffices that they have believed the Messiah has come. - Can it be that there are Rabbis who are cryptoChristians? - Rabbis who are crypto-Christians? Can any one who has beco me a Christian remain a Rabbi? That is, a Rabbi who teaches his fellow Jews that the Messiah has not yet come and, when they are dying, to whisper in their ear that the Messiah has come?
The 666 sea/ - Geronda, how soon will these events take place? - They are delayed because of you and because of me,

\ 202 SPIRITUAL BRAVE:RY so we may be able to acquire a good spiritual state. God is being patient, because if they were to occur now you and I would be los t. N owhere in the teaching of Christ is there a specific reference to time. 6 However, Sacred Scripture does say that the signs ofthe times wiJI wam us oftheir coming. 7 We must always be ready and shall see,¡, them when the time nears. Then we will be more certain.

' Time and experience wil/ reveal (the events) to those who are vigilant. 8 A book carne to my attention, which had on its cover three Jarge sixes! This was done to present the six nicely and to familiarize people with it. In time, little by little, the sea! will come as well. - Geronda, even the fasteners sown on clothes are so id in packages that bear the number 666. - That devil is at work everywhere! That number has been on credit cards for sorne time. Now it is on the fasteners! Many use the 666 as a trademark to sell more of their products. One supports the other. In other words, one 666 company wiJI prefer another 666 company. It has been written that when the depiction of a snake biting its tail is published, this will mean that the Jews have taken control of the world. For now, they have put it on a few currency bilis. The number 666 has already been a hit in China, in India! - Geronda, how do people know about using this number? - Saint John the Evangelist knew what the devil was

G. 106:340C. 1-~;<,..t The Signs uf the Times 203 planning to do, just as the Prophets foretold that Christ would be betrayed for thirty pieces of silver, 9 that He would be given vinegar to drink, 10 that His garrnents would be divided. 11 Two thousand years ago he wrote in the Book of Revelation that people would be marked with the number 666. He that hath understanding, let him count the number of the beast; for it is a number of a man; and his number is six hundred and sixty and six. 12 For the Jews the number 666 is a symbol of economy.

As mentioned in the Old Testament, the Israelites imposed a specific tax upon the people they conquered through various wars. The annual amount ofthe tax was 666 talents of gold. 13 Now, to subdue the whole world, they are again imposing this taxation number, which is connected with their glorious past. That's why they don 't want to replace it with another number. In other words, the number 666 is the symbol ofMammon. They borrowed it from the weights of goldnot knowing what Saint John says about it in the Book of Revelation - but, nevertheless, it still refers to Mammon. The Gospel says it clearly: Either Christ or Mammon. You cannot serve God and Mammon. 14 Events are progressing accordingly. In America, marked dogs transmit a signa! and can be located in no time at all wherever they might be. So they know where each dog is at. Dogs that do not have an identifying mark,
204 SPIRITUAL BRAVERY and are found without an owner are killed by laser. Later they'll start killing people, too.

Tons offish ha ve been marked and are being observed through satellites to study their migration pattems. And now a vaccine has been developed to combat a new disease, which will be obligatory and those taking it will be marked. Many people there are already marked with sorne sort of laser beam, on the forehead, on the arm.

Later on, anyone who is not marked with the number 666 will not be able to either buy or sell, to get a loan, to get a job, and so forth. My thinking te lis me that this is the system through which the Antichrist has chosen to take over the whole world, and people who are not part of this system won't be able to find work and so on, - whether black or white or red; in other words, everyone he will take over through an economic system that controls the global economy, and only those who have accepted the sea!, the mark of the number 666, will be able to participate in business dealings.
But what will become ofthe people who are sealed!

An expert was telling me that laser beams are harmful to people. People who are marked will attract the rays of the sun and suffer such harm that they will gnaw their tongues in pain. 15 Those who are not sealed will have a better fate than the others, because Christ will help those who are not sealed. And this is no small matter. - Geronda, when will Christ help them? Later? -No, right then. - Geronda, if they are not able to buy and sell, how will they be better off? l5.Cf.Rev 16:10.

''k ¡;,:. 1: Thc Signs of the Times 205 - You see, God has a way, and I know something about it. Well, this matter has preoccupied me a great deal and then He sent me the... telegram. Oh how God provides for us indeed! - Geronda, why is the sea! also called an inscription or engraving? - Because the sea! will not be superficial. What does "engraving" mean? Does it not mean deeply incised straight lines? The sea! will be a slash, placed first on al! the products and then in time, with laser beams, it will be enforced on people, too, on their hand or their forehead. Two years ago I spoke with a doctor from Toronto about this sealing and now he tells me that, according to a newspaper article he read, instead of an ID card, they will require sorne sort of impression on the hand. 16 Things are progressing, but we cannot exactly say that this or that will be done.

Again, certain television sets, which have recently been imported into Greece, have a componen!, which can observe those watching the television. Soon!hose who have a television will be watching their television, and, at the same time, they themselves will be watched! That is, those watching will be watched. Their whole life, what they say, what they do, everything, will be monitored through computers. Do you see what a dictatorship the devil has devised! In Brussels, there is an entire building with the 666, where they house the computer. That computer can monitor billions of people - the entire population ofthe world is approximately six billion. Confession for everyone at the push of a button! Sorne Europeans

206 SPIRITUAL BRAVERY have resisted these developments because they fear a global dictatorship. We, the Orthodox Christians, resist these developments, because we do not want the Antichrist nor the dictatorship, of course. Serious events are awaiting us, but they will not stand for long. Just as Orthodox Christianity was not wiped out by Communism and other enemies in the past, so it will not be wiped out now.

The New ID Cards - Geronda, someone said, "We use the five-thousand drachma currency note which bears the number 666, don't we? It's just going to be the same with the new identification cards." - The five-thousand drachma note is currency - and the English gold sovereign bears the image of Queen Victoria; this doesn't bother me. Render therefore unto Caesar the things that are Caesar 's." But this is about my identification card. It is something personal; it is not a mere currency note. Identification means what the word says: a person is identified personally by what that card states. The card contains the devil and 1 sign that 1 accept it. How can 1 do that? - Geronda, what is the relationship between the ID card and the sea!? - The ID card is not the sea!; it is the introduction to the sea!. - Geronda, people are asking what to do about the new ID cards. - When people ask you, it is better to advise them toj

The Signs of the Times 207 consult with their Spiritual Fathers and to be patient until we see how the Church responds to this matter. Many are asking questions, but few understand the answers. Since I have written about this clearly in the pamphlet The Signs of the Times, Jet each act according to his conscience. A few people have said of course, "Well, that is the opinion of one monk; it is not the Church 's position." But I did not express my own opinion; I simply stated the words of Christ, of the Gospel, because our opinion must be subject to the will of God as recorded in the Gospel. Others, who say the opposite of what I said, claim that Father Paisios said it. Still others who hear such things, seeing that they are such very serious matters, do not bother to ask if that is what 1 really said, but just believe whatever they hear. I am not afraid to speak the truth openly. Sorne people come to my Ka/yvi and throw sixes in the box. That is nothing, after all. But once they threw a sign outside my door. I thought someone had come and not found me and had written a sign, "He's out," so others would see it. Then I looked and saw the most abusive words, which I had not heard even as a layman. This whole present situation will be swept away, but first we will go through stormy times. People are roused and we, too, mus! rise up with much prayer.
Sorne people are concemed over the matter of the ID cards, while others are exploiting the issue in order to create problems. The Church must take a correct position.

She must speak out and explain to the faithful so that they will understand that, if they accept the new ID, it will amount to a spiritual fall. The Church must also ask the State to at least not make the new ID cards obligatory. If the Church takes a serious position, and the State respects the freedom of choice ofthe faithful, then whoever wants

208 SPIRITUAL BRAVERY can have a new ID card, and those who want to keep the old one can do so. In this case, the strong-willed who keep the o id card will have to endure the abuse of the many who will oppose them. Most people will just go about their business. Those who wish to be served will have the new cards, and the other poor people, the devout, will have the old identification card and will have to endure hardships.

Now that the minister has promised not to place the 666 on the new ID cards, visibly or invisibly, that, too, is something. We'll be patient and things will clear up in time. It is something even to say that they will not put the 666 on the cards. They themselves deny it, but we'll see what they do in the end. By the time the new ID cards come out, it is entirely possible that we may have the wrath of God upon us. After all, it is not likely that everyone will ha veto have a new ID card within twentyfour hours. Let's wait to see the first ones that come out.

They will be examined, and if the minister reneges on his word, then the struggle will be a righteous one. If we continue the demonstrations now, they will claim, "Look, they are shouting and creating trouble without reason." A dog, if it is a good guard dog, will bark when the thief comes. When the thiefleaves, the dog stops barking. If it continues to bark, then it is not a good guard dog. - Geronda, it was also said by the authorities that since we have religious tolerance in our country, the religion of each person should not be written on the new ID cards. -Yes, this does not interest them, but it certainly interests me, because this is what iáentifies me. The card must state where 1 am from and what I am. If religion is not,,: '. '..
The Signs uf the Times 209 included in the card there will be problems. For example, someone will go to the town hall for a marriage license.

If his ID card says "Orthodox"- regardless of how good a Christian he really is - then it will be alright. But if it does not state the religion, how can they give out a marriage license? For the Church this will become a complicated issue. Now, if religion is stated on an optional basis, this would also be a sort of confession. Europe is Europe. Here things are different.

The lnsidious Way of lntroducing the Sea/ Little by little, after the credit card and the ID card, that is, after the personal "record" of each person, they will proceed insidiously to the seal. Through various cunning means, they will compel people to accept the sea! on the forehead or on the arm. They will force the issue and say, "You can only use the card; currency will be abolished." People will give their card to the stores to buy what they need, and the merchant will get his money from the bank. Whoever does not have a card will not be able to bu y or sell. On the other hand, they will begin promoting "the other more perfect system" by marking every person with the 666, using laser beams on the forehead or the arm, which will not be visible on the outside.

At the same time, they will show on television that so and so stole the card of so and so and took all his money from the bank. And then they will add, 'The most secure system is to have the sea! on our forehead or arm with a laser beam, because only the bearer of this sea! will know his or her own personal number. Sealing is the most secure system. No one can take our head or our arm, nor will

\ \ 210 SPIRITUAL BRAVERY anybody be able to see the sea] embedded in us." That's why they Jet thieves and other criminals to rampage. Fifteen monastic cells were robbed near Karyes. One person was killed while being robbed. So, people will find an opportunity to vio late laws and traditions and take whatever they want. Let us say, for example, that someone wants to encroach on somebody's field and claims that it once belonged to his grandfather who had rented it to sorne shepherds as a pasture for their sheep, and you cannot verify this. Then, the authorities will say, "Unfortunately, as it is, we cannot check the facts; we can only do this with the computer." And then they will proceed with the sealing. The computer will then do the search to find out whether or not someone is or is not marked and sealed so the(call b~hetped witlr · roblem or not.,,\: · 'f!:¡e three and a half years 18 w1. be diflicult, during C' which tlle·few.who.OO lltlt agwoWith this system will endure hardships. Finally the authorities will say, "We're sorry, you are innocent after all. If only you had been sealed, we would have been able to gather the necessary information in a minute. Now we cannot check the facts." - Geronda, will they be able to impose the sea! on everybody by force? - They are too "refined" todo that! They'll be courteous about itafter all, they are Europeans! They will display •

Thc Signs of the Times 211 sorne superiority. They won't torture people but anyone who is not sealed will simply not be able to live normally in society. They will then again say, "Your troubles come from not being sealed; ifyou accept the sea!, you will not have these difliculties." If any one happens to have gold sovereigns or dollars, he will not be able to use them.

So if you can now leam to live simply and frugally, yo u will be able to get through those diflicult years. lt will be helpful to have a small field, to cultivate sorne wheat or potatoes; to have a few olive trees, and then with sorne farro animals, a goat, a few chickens you will be able to provide for the needs ofyour family. For even ifyou were to stockpile various groceries, this would not do because such foods do not keep; they spoil quickly. Naturally, these diflicult times will be short, three to three and a halfyears. 19 The time will be shortened for the sake ofthe elect. 20 They will not realize how quickly the time will pass. God will not leave roan without help. - Geronda, will Christ intervene during these diflicult years? -Yes. The Saints, Panaghia, Christ will appear to save even one wronged roan with a good disposition, because he deserves divine help, and even more so when the whole world will be going through such a diflicult time.

Now it will be a passing storm, a short occupation by the Antichrist Salan. He will then be given such a slap by Christ, all the nations will be shaken up and then peace will prevail in the world for a long time. During this time, Christ will provide the opportunity for His creation to be

212 SPIRITUAL BRAVERY saved. ls Christ going to abandon His creation? No, He will appear when mankind has reached an impasse and will save it from the clutches of the Antichrist. People will retum to Christ and a spiritual serenity will prevail over al! the earth for many years. Sorne people associate the Second Coming of Christ with this intervention of His. My thinking tells me that this will not be the Second Coming of Christ when He comes as Judge, but only an intervention of Christ, for there are so many events that have not taken place.

Christ will intervene, slap down this whole system, prevail over al! evil and transform it into good in the end. The roads will be filled with many shrines of devotion. The buses will have lcons on their billboards. Al! the people will believe, and will want you to tell them about Jesus Christ! Thus the Gospel will be proclaimed throughout the whole world, 21 and then Christ will come as Judge to judge the world. Judgment is one thing and intervention to help His creation is another.

Sealing Equals Denial While the Evangelist John clearly refers to the ingrained se al in the Book of Revelation, 22 sorne people just do not understand. What can you tell them? Unfortunately, one hears so many foolish ideas of the brain from certain "Gnostics" ofthe day. One says, "l will accept the ID card with the number 666 and will place a cross on it." Another says, "I will accept the seal on the forehead
The Signs of the Times 213 but will puta cross on my forehead as well" and so many other similar foolish notions. They think that such things sanctify, when in fact they are spiritual errors.

A bishop told me, "Next to the spot where I will sign my name, l will also put the sign of the cross. I am not denying Christ, l am only trying to serve my needs." T told him, "You being a bishop, it's alright for you to sign your name with a cross, and he, being an archimandrite priest, can al so sign his name with a cross; but what will the poor people do?" What is unclean cannot be sanctified. Clean water is receptive of Grace and is sanctified.

Urine cannot become holy water. A stone may be tumed into bread through a miracle. But an unclean thing is not receptive of sanctification. As such, the devil, Antichrist, when he is on our ID card or on our forehead or our arm with his symbol, cannot be sanctified by placing a cross there. We have the power ofthe Precious Cross, the Holy Symbol, the divine Grace of Christ, and only when we preserve the Grace of our Holy Baptism, through which we deny Satan, are we united to Christ and receive the Holy Sea!, The Sea/ of the Gift of the Holy Spirit. Do yo u see how they are trying to proceed with rational thinking... puta cross next to their name and everything will be al! right! And while we see that the Apostle Peter extemally denied Christ, and this, too, was a denial, 23 these rationalists deny the Holy Sea! ofChrist which was given to them in Holy Baptism, by accepting the seal of the Antichrist and even claiming that they still have Christ in them!! - Geronda, what happens if someone accepts the seal out of ignorance?

214 SPIRITUAL BRAVERY - lt will be out of indifference, not ignorance. How can there be ignorance when things are so clear? And even if someone does not know, he mus! be interested to find out and to leam. If we claim that we did not know and for this reason accepted the sea], then Christ will tell us stemly, O ye hypocrites, ye can discern the face of the sky, but can ye not discern the signs of the times.24 When even out of ignorance someone receives the sign, he loses the di vine Grace and receives the infiuence of demonic energy. You see, when the child is immersed in the baptismal water by the priest during Holy Baptism, he receives the Holy Spirit without knowing it, and di vine Grace dwells in him from then on.

Jnterpreting the Prophecies - Geronda, sorne people say, "Whatever is written by God, that is what will happen. Why then should we be concemed?" - Yes, they say that, my child, but it is not how it is!

I, too, hear sorne people saying, "The Jews are not such fools as to be betrayed by the 666, when the Evangelist John writes about it in the Book of Revelation. If they were, they would have done it in a smarter, more secretive manner." But can we say that the Scribes and Pharisees did not know the OId Testament? Didn'tAnnas and Caiaphas know better!han anyone that in the O Id Testament it was written that the Messiah would be betrayed for thirty pieces of silver? 25 Why didn't they ask for thirty-one

The Signs of the Times 215 or twenty-nine and instead asked for "thirty"? But they were blind. God knew that things would happen in this way. God foreknows but does not predestine - only the Turks believe in what is written, in kismet. God knows that something will happen in a particular way, but man still does it out of bis foolishness. 1t is not a matter of God having made a specific decision, but He sees the evil deeds of men and where it willlead them, and knows that their minds will not change. Again, it is not a matter of God arranging things to happen in a particular manner.

Other people are preoccupied with prophecies and devise their own interpretation. They do not say, at leas!, "This is my opinion about this matter," but they state unequivocally, "This is how it is," and then go on to relate many theories of their own. Sorne will interpret the prophecies to their liking in order to justif)r their passions. An example of this is the statement made by Saint Cyril, lt would be better if the signs of the Antichrist did not take place in our times. 26 Someone who wants to justify himself, bis cowardice, will say, "Ah, you see? Saint Cyril was afraid he might deny Christ if he had to face the dangers of the Antichrist; am I any better than Saint Cyril? Therefore, even if 1 should deny Christ under such conditions, it is of no consequence... " Of course, Saint Cyril said that the events should not happen so that bis eyes would not see the Antichrist, and not that he was afraid ofhim. Do you see what the devil can do?
Again certain "Gnostics", unfortunately, wrap up their spiritual children with swaddling-bands as if they were

216 SPIRITUAL BRAVERY infants, presumably to protect them from worrying. 'This does not matter; that is not important. As long as you believe inside... " Or they may advise them, "Do not talk about this subject - about the ID cards, the sea! -, so as not to upset people." On the contrary, if they tell them, "Let's try to live more spiritually; let's be close to Christ; don 't be afraid of anything; and in the final reckoning we'll go as true witnesses and martyrs," then at least they will be better prepared. When we know the truth, we are given the opportunity to be troubled, to ask questions. We are pained by the present condition, we pray more fervently, and we are careful not to fall into a spiritual trap.

But now what? Besides giving their own interpretations, sorne are also fearful like worldly people, when these spiritual teachers should be troubled spiritually over the situation and should be helping the Christians by instilling in them a positive concern and by strengthening their faith to find di vine consolation. I ask myself: are they not troubled by all these events which are taking place in our society and in our time? Why don't they put even one question mark against the interpretations of their mind? And if they themselves render indirect assistance to the Antichrist regarding the sea!, how can they mislead other souls toward perdition? When Sacred Scripture says, To lead astray, if possib/e, the elect,27 it means that the people who try to interpret these matters with their own mind can readily go astray into error.

Therefore behind the perfect system of "the service card", the security computer, the sinister global dictator27. Mk 13:22.,.! j j The Signs of the Times 217 ship is lurking, the Antichrist's slavery. And he causeth al/, the smal/ and the great, and the rich and the poor, and the free and the bond, that there be given them a mark on their right hand, or upon their.forehead; and that no man should be able to buy orto se//, save he that hath the mark, even the name ofthe beast or the number o.f his name. Here is wisdom: He that hath understanding, let him count the number o.f the beast;.for it is the number of a man; and his number is six hundred and sixty and six. 28

CHAPTERTWO Sacrifice Brings Joy Nowadays Sacrijice ls Rare woman who had everything told me once that having children is a dizzying bother. She couldn 't be bothered to be a mother! When a mother thinks like that, she becomes useless, for mothers, after all, are supposed to have!ove naturally. Before having her own family, a young woman may be woken up by her mother at ten in the morning. But from the moment she becomes a mother and has to feed and bathe her child, she won 't even sleep at night, beca use the heart starts working. When a person has a sense of sacrifice, he does not complain, he is not lazy; he rejoices. That's the key, to have a spirit of sacrifice. Ifthat woman had said, "Oh Lord, how can I thank you? You have not only given me children, but also many material goods... So many people have nothing and I have houses, I have property from my father, my husband earns a good salary, and I receive two more salaries from rents, and I have an easy life! How can I thank you my God? I am not worthy of these things." lf she thought like this, then by praising God she would stop feeling sorry for herself. In other S~criticc Brings Joy 219 words, it would have been enough if she had only been gratefu 1 to God. - Geronda, sacrifice gives us joy. -Oh, whatjoy it brings! Nowadays people don't savour this joy of sacrifice, and this is why they are tormented.
They have no ideals in them; they are too bored to live.
A generous heart and self-denial are what drive us.

Without this force, we are tormented. In the old days, in the villages, sorne people would go out at night unseen to quietly open up a pathway for others to bless and forgive them when they died. Now one seldom encounters this spirit of sacrifice.

I remember a festive procession around the Monastery on the Holy Mountain. As the monks walked close by a large bramble bush, their monastic veils would get snagged on a branch. Not one ofthem stopped for a minute to break it off and get it out of the way for the sake of the others behind; instead, each stooped under it to avoid getting snagged. Were they bowing to the bramble bush? Ifit were the Sacred Bramble Bush, then it would ha ve been appropriate. People tend to think, "Let someone else take care ofthe problem, l'lljust do my own work." But why don't you take care of it, since you were the first to notice? That is what worldly people who do not believe in God do. I have no use for such a life; 1 would rather die a thousand deaths. The goal is for each one of us to think of the other person, of the other person 's pain.

People seem to have lost control. The sense of philotimo, of sacrifice, is gone. 1 have often told you how it was with me then with the hernia at the Kalyvi... When someone rang the bell on the door, 1 would go and open the door, even in the snow. lf someone had a serious

220 SPIRITUAL BRAVERY problem, then 1 would not even feel my own pain, even though I had previously been sick enough for bed. I would also offer them sorne treat, holding the treat with one hand and the hernia with the other. Throughout our conversation, even though the pain was severe, I would not lean on anything so as not to reveal my discomfort.

When the visitor left, I would again collapse from the pain. It was not that before my own pain had passed or that I had been miraculously cured, but, in feeling the other person's pain, I forgot my own. The miracle happens when someone can be compassionate and feel the other's pain. The whole idea here is to be able to see the other person as a brother and feel his pain. It is this very pain that moves God and brings about the miracle. For there is nothing else which moves God as much as a noble and sacrificial spirit. But now, in our time, this kind of nobility is rare, because self-love and self-interest have entered the picture. Seldom does someone say, "Let me give my tum, my place, to someone else, and it's alright if 1 am delayed." Few are those blessed souls who think of others. E ven among the spiritual people, there is a contrary spirit, a spirit of indifference.

The good is good only when the one who does it sacrifices something from himself, sorne sleep, sorne rest and so on. That is why Christ said, But she ofher penury hath cast in al/ the living that she had.' When I am at ease and do sorne good, it does not have the same value.
But when I am tired and someone asks me, let's say, for directions and I do it, then it has value. Or, when I have had plenty of sleep and stay up all night with someone ' l. Cf. Lk21:4; Mk 12:44.

Sacrifice Brings JO)' 221 who needs help, this does not have great value. And if I'm fond of conversation, I may do this in order to enjoy the company and be entertained a little. Whereas, when 1 am tired and make a sacrifice to help someone, then 1 experience a heavenly joy. It is then that I am bombarded with the blessing of God!

When someone is too lazy not only to help others, but even to do something for himself, then he can gct tired while resting. One who helps rests through tiring himself. One who has the spirit of sacrifice, will, for example, if he sees someone physically weak working and tiring himself, tell him, "sit down and rest", and do the work. The weak person will get physical rest, but the other will experience spiritual rest. Whatever one does he must do it with his heart, otherwise he is not transformed spiritually. Whatever is done with the heart does not tire us. The heart is like a machine that is charged; the more it works, the more it is charged. You see, the chain saw, when it finds a soft log, goes "vrrrrr... " and stops; but when it finds a hard log, it forces itself, gets overcharged and really works effectively. And, not only when we givc, but also when we are about to receive something, let us not think of ourselves, but let us loo k to see what will be of sorne comfort to the other person. Let us not be greedy and insatiable, believing that we are entitled to take all we want, leaving nothing for others. - Geronda, again it is the spirit of sacrifice that enters into the situation. - But in the spiritual life the whole basis is there. Can you imagine what joy is experienced by the one who sacrifices himself? One cannot even express the joy he feels. Sublime joy emanates from sacrifice. Only when

222 SPIRITUAL BRAVERY we sacrifice ourselves can we be related to Christ, for Christ is sacrifice. Man can live in Paradise from here and now, or he can live in hell. Whoever does good is overjoyed, for he is rewarded with divine consolation.
Whoever does evil suffers.

My Repose Stems From the Repose ofOthers - Geronda, if someone has not!asted the joy of sacrifice, how can he attain such a spirit of sacrifice? - By putting himself in the other's place. When l was serving in the army, the machine-gun post was often filled with water; the batteries for the wireless needed to be changed - and it was very difficult, because the line was overloaded. l was drenched to my waist and my overcoat was dripping. Yet, l preferred to do the work myself so as not to trouble the others, and l was happy to do it. M y commanding officer would tell me, "l am at ease and feel secure when you do the work, but 1 feel sorry for you. Let someone else go and do the work." But 1 would tell him, "No, 1 am happy to do it, sir." There was another wireless operator in our company, but during the manoeuvres, 1 didn't Jet him carry the batteries or the wireless, even though they were heavy, so that he wouldn't be in danger.

He pleaded with me, "Why don'! you Jet me carry them?" And l would tell him, "You have a wife and children.lfyou are killed, l will have to answer to God." So God protected both of us; He didn 't allow either of us to get killed.

It is preferable for a sensitive person to die once out of ]ove in order to protect his neighbour, rather than be • neglectful or cowardly and then to be constan ti y tormented by his conscience for the rest of his Ji fe.

Sacrifice Rrings Joy 223 Once, during the guerrilla warfare manoeuvres, the guerrillas had cut us off outside one of the villages, and the soldiers were going to draw lots to see who would go into the village for supplies. ''1'11 go," l said. If sorne inexperienced or careless soldier had to go, he could have been killed and then my conscience would ha ve troubled me. "It would be better," I thought, "for me to die than the other soldier, and then to have my conscience killing me for the res! ofmy life. How could l endure that? My conscience would tell me, 'You could have saved him; why didn't you save him?'" At the time, I was fasting and had not eaten... but never mind that. Our commanding officer told me, "I, too, prefer that you go, for you are experienced, but you should eat something to have energy." 1 took my weapon and set off. The guerrillas mistook me for one of their men and allowed me to pass through. l went into the village and entered a two-storied house. An elderly woman who was there gave me supplies and l retumed to my company safely.

The greatest joy I experienced in the winter, there in the snow. 1 remember one night waking up, while the others were still sleeping. The snow had covered the tents. 1 went, too k the wireless, removed the snow from its boles and saw that it still worked. 1 ran to our commanding officer and told him. That night 1 saved twenty-six soldiers from freezing by digging them out of the snow with a shovel. I have done nothing for Christ. If only ten percent of what 1 did in the army had be en done for Christ, now I would be doing miracles! This is why later as a monk I would say, "While in the army 1 went through so many hardships for my country, but now as a monk what am 1 doing?" In other words, compared to the hardships

224 SPIRITUAL BRAVERY 1 endured in the arrny, my life as a monk was like being a princeregardless if I had or didn't have even a crust of bread to eat. Have you any idea what kind of fasting we did during the arrny manoeuvres? We ate granulated snow. The others ran about and found something to eat.

But I could not move about with the wireless. Once we were without food for thirteen days. They had rationed to us one loaf of bread and half a salted herring. Sometimes the only thing we had to drink was the muddy water in the hoof prints of the animals. Most of the time, 1 had to quench my thirst not with clean rainwater, but with muddy water. Once I even drank sorne... "orange juice"!
Later as a monk, even the water that may not have been so pure seemed to me like a great blessing. At least it looked like water.
Once one aftemoon, the ground line had been broken.
It was December of 1948. The snow was deep. At four o'clock in the aftemoon, the order was given to go to the village two hours away, to repair the line and retum.

In the meantime, there were only two more hours of daylight. The soldiers were dead ti red and didn 't have the energy to start. And how was one to find the broken line in so much snow! - Didn't you know the way and where the line was? - Well, I had an idea where the road was, but the night was fast approaching. In any case, they gave me a group of soldiers and we started out. At first we cleared the snow from the road in the camp and proceeded a little to set our commanding officer at ease. Then l said, "Let's move on now, for we must also retum." 1 took the lead because the othets were resisting. They were grumbling, "Greece never dies... but we are dying." The same thing Sacrifice Brings_Joy 225 over and over again. Again and again, 1 would sink into the snow, and they would lift me up. 1 carried a bayonet which I would thrust into the snow to create a grounding. I had to check constantly. I would get in the front. "Move on, animals don't pass this way to cut the line. Only in sorne depression in the ground where the line may be suspended over it, there is where we should check." Finally, we reached a village with terraces, but because the wind had made snow drifts there, we could not see anything. When we reached the terraces, 1 fell into the snowdrift and the others had a hard time getting me out. Gradually, going from one terrace to the next, don't even ask me how, we all descended to the village late at night. In sorne nearby depressions I found the broken line dangling in two places; we repaired it and were able to communicate with our commander. He told us, "Retum to the camp." But how were we to retum to the camp? Aside from it being night time, how were we to climb the terraces, from which we virtually had just rolled down. How were we to find our way at night? So 1 told him, "We made it downhill, but now it will be very difficult going uphill. Let us retum tomorrow moming by going around the village." He insisted that we retum that night. Fortunately, an adjutant heard the commander and pleaded with him to allow us to remain in the village that night, which we did. We received hospitality in a house and they gave us severa! tserges, 2 to sleep in. I was completely soaked by shovelling the snow to open the way in front, so they felt sorry for me and placed me in the middle so that I could be warrn. During all that time,

I had to give you these examples so that you understand what sacrifice means. l didn't tell you all these things so that you'd applaud me, but so that you understand where true joy comes from. In addition to the above, sorne people in the Signals Office would tell me lies, "M y father is coming and l need to go and see him.
Please take over my duties for a while." Another would say, "My sister is here" - and she wasn 't even his sister.

Still others would say something else, and l would constantly be doing sorne service for one or another of my fellow soldiers and sacrificing myself. Then l would also sweep and clean, because it was forbidden for others to enter the Signals Company. Not even an officer from another service was permitted to enter, for it was wartime.

We could not even get someone to clean. So l would pick up the broom and clean up all the areas. It was there that I leamed to sweep. I simply thought to myself, "This, a place of service, andina way it is a sacred place; it cannot be untidy." I was not required to do the cleaning, nor did I know how. At home, I had never touched a broom, and, ifl had, my sister would have taken it from me and given me a beating with it. The others called me "the cleaning woman" or "perpetua! victim". But I never minded these things. And l never did anything to hear a "thank yo u". l really did it with my heart, because I felt it was necessary, and I enjoyed it. - Geronda, did you have any negative thoughts? For example, did you think that fellow is just going out; he's not going to see his sister? - No, I didn't have such thoughts. From the moment Sacrifice Brings JOJ' 227 someone said to me, "Please, can you take my place for a while," that was it; it was done. Sometimes someone would ask me for money and tell me it was for his children. He not only did not send the money to his children, but asked for more from his wife to spend on himself. You see? Nor did 1 do it to eam praise. l simply felt itas a need. Because l never took any lea ve, the others too k advantage of this and left their work forme to do. I ended up doing all the company's work. The signals were piling up, and the lines were constantly busy. l would get wom out. Once I hada temperature of39.5 C. and didn't tell anyone. Finally, I fainted from exhaustion and they took me away on a stretcher to a hospital. l heard them saying, "Come on Benedictos, 3 we are taking you with the stretcher to the garage for overhaul!" They left me there alone - who would pay any attention to me? Their concem was for the wounded -, but I felt joy, the joy which comes from sacrifice. For l found my repose in the repose of others.

But whoever thinks only ofhimself is isolated from others as well as from God-a double isolationin which case he cannot

Someone is tested through the trials oflife. It is there that you see if he has real!ove, a true spirit of sacrifice.

And when we say that someone has the spirit of sacrifice, we mean that at the time of danger he does not consider himself but thinks of the others. When even now someone does not think ofhimselfbut is thoughtful and considerate of others, he will do the same ata time of danger. It is Sacrifice Brings Joy_ ___ ____ _ 229 then that it becomes clear who are truly the people with a sacrificial spirit and who are the selfish ones.

If one does not start sacrificing something now, like giving up sorne desire or selfishness, how will he ever be able to sacrifice his life ata difficult time? If, even now, he thinks of the labour and tries to avoid working a little harder than the next person, how will he ever attain the state of risking his own life to save another's? If now he thinks only of himself even in small matters, how can he then think of others when his own life is in danger? Then it will be far more difficult. If difficult times come and a selfish person sees a neighbour, for example, sick with fever collapsing in the street, he willleave him there and go away saying, "! must go and lie down to rest lest, 1, too, become sick." During wartime, your own life is struggling with the life ofthe other. Bravery is running to save another's life.

When there is no spirit of sacrifice, everyone looks to only save himself. And it is often seen that someone who during the war tries to hide is most often found by a hand grenade. He attempts to escape and he comes a cropper.

For this reason, one must not try to escape, especially at the expense of others. 1 remember an incident during the Albanian war. A soldier had a stone slab to protect his head. In the meantime, he had to move over and he set the slab down. Immediately the soldier next to him took it, saying, "Here's my chance to take it for myselfnow." At that moment, a mortar fell on him and killed him. He saw the incoming fire and too k the slab for himself without thinking of the other soldier who would retum. He thought only ofhimself and justified his action. "Since the
230 SI'IRITUAL BRAVERY other soldier moved over a little, I can now take his slab for myself." Yes, he left, but it was his protective slab.
Another person tried throughout the duration of the war to save himself. He thought of no one but himself.

The others helped, but he stayed at home. He sought until the very end, when things became very difficult, to avoid danger. Later, when the British had come, he went to the camp, presented himself to Napoleon Zervas 4 and, because he al so had American citizenship, found an opportunity to leave for America. As soon as he arrived there he died! His poor wife used to say, "He tried to escape from God!" He died, while others who went to war survived.

Those Who Die Bravely Do Not Die 1 remember that in the arrny we all had a common goal. I tried, but the spirit of sacrifice existed in the others as well, regardless of whether or not they believed in the afterlife. "Why should he be killed? He is a family man," they would say, and they would volunteer for a dangerous mission. The sacrifice they made had greater value than the sacrifice made by a believer. The faithful person believes in di vine justice and divine recompense, whereas the others do not know their sacrifice will not be in vain and will have its recompense in the afterlife.

He Who Does Not Consider Himse(f Receives Divine Power - Geronda, was your life ever in danger during the war? - Oh yes, not just once or twice. I think now how God helped and I'm profoundly moved. I didn't think of it then. Especially death, l didn 't think about it at all.

When you are determined to face death, you aren't afraid of anything. The determination for death is equal to a thousand guards. Death is security. In war, the sacred companies of dedicated soldiers have the skull as their symbol; it says that they are ready to die. Whoever doesn 't think of himself but prefers the good of others and the general good, receives divine power. And you see, when someone acts with a sacrificial spirit, God protects him.

I remember once we set up a defensive post behind a huge boulder. I had dug a foxhole and was hiding in it. A fellow soldier carne and asked me, "May I come in too?" Another soldier carne and asked the same thing. I allowed them to come into my foxhole and I remained outside.

At night when many bullets were being fired, one literally grazed my scalp. 1 was wearing only a hood, not the helmet. I shouted, "Guys, I've been hit!" I touched my head with my hand and Jooked, but there was no blood. 1 touched it again, nothing. The bulle! had grazed my head
! 11 234 SPIRITUAL BRAVERY and had only cut my hair toward the back, leaving a strip six centimetres wide without any hair!
You have not gone through difficult years or occupations; you have not seen wars or enemies, and so onand I hope you never donor do you understand such things.
But these years are like a pressure-cooker that is boiling and whistling. It takes endurance, bravery and manliness.

If something should happen, be sure not to leave yourselves completely unprepared. Be prepared from now to be able to face any potential difficulty. What did Christ say? Didn't He say, Be ye ready? 8 Living in such difficult times as we do today gives us one more reason to be all the more prepared. It is not only sudden death that we may encounter; there are other dangers as well. Therefore, dispel the spirit of ease and comfort for ourselves.
Let the sense of philotimo prevail. May you always have the spirit of sacrifice.
Now I see something that is about to happen, but is constantly postponed. There are many short postponements. Who is making these delays? Is God pushing?

Let's give it one more month, two months! This is how the present situation is going. 9 But because we do not know what is waiting for us, it is best to cultivate as much as possible the virtue of!ove. This is the most important of all: to have true!ove among yourselves, sisterly!ove, not false love. Always, when there is true concem for each other, compassion and!ove, one can act correctly.
Kindness and love are empowering. As far as possible, be discreet in your conversations and do not overly open ' 8. Mt 24:44; Lk 12:40.

Sacrifice Brings.loy 235 up to others. One will say it to another and another to yet another, and then what will come of that? It is entirely possible from one foolish remark to cause a lot ofharm ' and then you'll be knocking your heads on the wall. You should have seen the secrecy observed in the army! Your first responsibility if captured was to destroy the secret signals code. You had to cut it into small pieces and swallow them. Once, when I was in danger ofbeing captured, I swallowed them. Otherwise, if the guerrillas had taken them and had leamed the army was in a particular place, needing food or supplies, they could then send a message to our headquarters, and the air force would be dispatched to drop food for them and bombs on our army.

You see? The guerrillas could present themselves as the army. If you were captured and they knew you were the wireless operator, they would pul! out your fingemails with pliers to make you tell them the secret signals code.

And you were willing to endure that torture rather than to betray the secret code. Another soldier was bumed under the armpits to deliver a particular secret letter. He did not give in and became paralysed from the torture. He did not confess and that is why he became a confessor.
Sorne women transported secret communications hidden in saddles and were determined to die if necessary.

Death in wartime is very redeeming when someone sacrifices himself in order to protect others. Those who sacrifice their life from pure!ove in order to protect their fellow human being are imitating Christ Himself. They are the greatest heroes, because they are feared even by death itself, as out oflove they defy death and gain immortality, taking the key of etemity right out from under the slab of the tomb and trip lightly toward eterna! blessedness.

236 SPIRITUAL BRAVERY The Entire LiJe ofa Monk Is a Sacrijice The monk is helped naturally by his whole way of life to have!ove and sacrifice. He has set out to die for Christ. In other words, he set out for sacrifice. Having no obligations, the monk is obliged to cultivate the spirit of sacrifice. A layman does not set out to die for Christ. In addition, he has obligations; he must think of his family, his children, and for this he has extenuating circumstances, he can be justified. For example, during a war, one who has a family may try to avoid the danger, for the sake ofhis children. He may not think that, in doing so, someone else may die who al so has children. In any case, there is here at least a concem for his family. He says, "M y children must not be left on the street." He may not even believe in the other life and be only trying to save this life. -In other words, Geronda, a monk must be constantly sacrificing himself. - Loo k, didn 't we say that the whole life of a monastic person is one big sacrifice? Otherwise we achieve nothing.
If a monastic is making slow progress in this matter, then he is not a monastic. Then where is the spirituality?

There is no spirituality when there is no sacrifice. All the spiritual disciplines which a monk does without sacrificing himself are nothing. On the Holy Mountain, such aspects of the spiritual life, for example prayer, fasting, vigils, and so forth, are called koukouskiachtika (scarecrow). In other words, a monk who performs these spiritual disciplines cannot dispel the demons; at best he may dispel the crows. When someone takes seriously the struggle that must be carried out in this life, there is a di vine flame in him. Ifthis di vine flame is missing, then he Sacrific" Brings Jo y 237 is useless. It is this that gives him jo y, courage, philotimo.

This is what the Lord said, 1 carne to cast jire upon the earth.'" When this divine fire exists, then the chanting and the prayer we do for ourselves and for others will ha ve results. This is especially true for a woman 's heart which, when purified, has tremendous power. They also make great strides in prayer, becoming a radar for God.
Whoever has no philotimo, no sacrifice, will have either worldly joy or worldly worries, but he cannot experience spiritual delight.

This is why I say that sacrifice must be developed, the sense of brotherhood. Each one of you must acquire a spiritual state, in order to be able to get through things alone ata difficult time. If one is not in this spiritual state, he may falter because of self-love. He may even deny or betray Christ. You must be prepared to die. Here there are laypersons in the world who sacrifice themselves and don't even believe in Paradise. We believe that nothing is lost and our sacrifice has meaning. The holy Martyrs felt great joy when they had an opportunity to endure martyrdom. It was from this early martyrdom that asceticism had its beginning in the spiritual life. When Saint Constantine the Great carne to power in the Empire, he released the Christians from the prisonswhere they were being kept to gradually eliminate themand the persecutions ended. But those in the prisons, who were waiting with joy for their turn to become martyrs, were greatly disappointed when Constantine the Great altered the course of events. Just when they were expecting to be martyred, they were set free. Consequently, out of!ove for God and the burning desire to be martyred, they took to the mountains. Thus, the suffering they would have endured under Diocletian and Maximinian, they imposed upon themselves through their ascetic rigours. One would go, with hands tied to ropes, and hang from a tree, praying with pain but with di vine gladness.
